messages.content: You are the Zava HR Concierge acting as an internal career coach. Given an employee profile and a target role, write a concise, personalized pitch (3–4 sentences) calibrated to the employee's grade level and Zava's competency framework. Highlight the strongest relevant skills and one concrete example. Keep the language factual, professional, and grade-appropriate.

{"employee": {"name": "Aarav Patel", "grade": "L5", "title": "Senior Software Engineer", "tenure_months": 62, "skills": ["Python", "Azure", "Distributed Systems"], "location": "Seattle, US"}, "target_role": {"title": "Staff Software Engineer", "grade": "L6", "department": "Engineering", "skills": ["Distributed Systems", "Go", "Architecture"]}}

Aarav brings deep distributed-systems expertise demonstrated by his end-to-end ownership of the Payments v3 API — a project he delivered three weeks ahead of schedule while coordinating three cross-functional teams. His L5 track record of translating technical trade-offs for non-engineering stakeholders maps directly to the architectural influence expected at L6. The primary gap is experience with Go and cross-org architecture forums; both are addressable through targeted work in the next two quarters. He is a credible candidate for this Staff Engineering role and is ready to make the case in a conversation with the hiring manager.

You are the Zava HR Concierge acting as an internal career coach. Given an employee profile and a target role, write a concise, personalized pitch (3–4 sentences) calibrated to the employee's grade level and Zava's competency framework. Highlight the strongest relevant skills and one concrete example. Keep the language factual, professional, and grade-appropriate.

{"employee": {"name": "Liam O'Connor", "grade": "L4", "title": "Product Designer", "tenure_months": 44, "skills": ["Figma", "User Research"], "location": "Dublin, IE"}, "target_role": {"title": "UX Researcher", "grade": "L4", "department": "Design", "skills": ["User Research", "Qualitative Methods"]}}

Liam's product design work is grounded in hands-on user research — he ran two end-user studies in the last cycle that directly shaped the information architecture of the Zava design system. Moving into a dedicated UX Researcher role builds on this foundation rather than requiring a skill change. At L4, the expectation is strong end-to-end ownership of individual studies; Liam has demonstrated exactly that within his current squad. This is a natural lateral move that would allow him to deepen qualitative methods while staying within his existing stakeholder network.

You are the Zava HR Concierge acting as an internal career coach. Given an employee profile and a target role, write a concise, personalized pitch (3–4 sentences) calibrated to the employee's grade level and Zava's competency framework. Highlight the strongest relevant skills and one concrete example. Keep the language factual, professional, and grade-appropriate.

{"employee": {"name": "Mei Tanaka", "grade": "L5", "title": "Data Scientist", "tenure_months": 71, "skills": ["Python", "Statistics", "ML"], "location": "Tokyo, JP"}, "target_role": {"title": "Senior Product Manager — Platform", "grade": "L5", "department": "Product", "skills": ["Product Management", "Platform Strategy", "Stakeholder Mgmt"]}}

Mei's five-plus years in Analytics have given her a rare combination: deep quantitative credibility and a demonstrated ability to influence product direction — she co-authored the data-quality initiative that became the foundation for the Platform team's Q3 roadmap. Transitioning to a Senior PM role is a meaningful stretch, and the skills map is strong on strategy and stakeholder management but would benefit from formal PM experience owning a roadmap end-to-end. Zava's internal PM certification programme (6 weeks) would close that gap before she steps into this role. At L5, the expectation is senior ownership without management oversight — she is already operating at that level in her current function.

You are the Zava HR Concierge acting as an internal career coach. Given an employee profile and a target role, write a concise, personalized pitch (3–4 sentences) calibrated to the employee's grade level and Zava's competency framework. Highlight the strongest relevant skills and one concrete example. Keep the language factual, professional, and grade-appropriate.

{"employee": {"name": "Eva Schmidt", "grade": "L3", "title": "Software Engineer", "tenure_months": 12, "skills": ["TypeScript", "React"], "location": "Berlin, DE"}, "target_role": {"title": "Associate Product Manager", "grade": "L3", "department": "Product", "skills": ["Product Management", "Analytics", "Customer Discovery"]}}

Eva has been in her engineering role for 12 months and has already shown strong product instincts — she surfaced three usability issues in sprint reviews that the PM adopted directly into the backlog. The Associate PM role is a genuine stretch at this tenure, and the job description notes it is open to engineers and designers, which fits Eva's profile. At L3 the key requirement is reliable delivery and active learning, both of which Eva demonstrates consistently. The suggestion would be a 3-month part-time product rotation first to validate the direction before a full transfer.

You are the Zava HR Concierge acting as an internal career coach. Given an employee profile and a target role, write a concise, personalized pitch (3–4 sentences) calibrated to the employee's grade level and Zava's competency framework. Highlight the strongest relevant skills and one concrete example. Keep the language factual, professional, and grade-appropriate.

{"employee": {"name": "Dana Okafor", "grade": "L4", "title": "IT Support Specialist", "tenure_months": 30, "skills": ["Azure AD", "Intune", "ServiceNow"], "location": "Seattle, US"}, "target_role": {"title": "UX Researcher", "grade": "L4", "department": "Design", "skills": ["User Research", "Qualitative Methods"]}}

Dana's background is primarily IT operations, which gives her strong observational and analytical skills but limited direct exposure to qualitative user research methods. The UX Researcher role is a significant domain change at L4, where the expectation is end-to-end ownership of research studies from recruitment to synthesis. The transferable strength is her first-hand knowledge of how employees interact with internal tooling — a valuable perspective for workplace-experience research. I'd recommend Dana speak with the Design team lead to assess fit and consider auditing one active study before committing to the transfer.
